raw_consumer_statement: I made a purchase with XXXX XXXX online during a XXXX special for a XXXX trial. The first charge was XXXX, and I would be billed the balance in 30 days. I was charged the full price of the item a week later, not 30 days. I ended up returning the item and requesting a refund. The merchant agreed to a full refund in an email to me. I only received the XXXX chargeback and never received the first charge of XXXX because the merchant disputed the refund and told the credit card company they didn't agree to a refund and they had no record of a refund. I had an email from them, and that wasn't enough. In addition, I had an email about the return. I was told I needed to contact the Merchant for the balance, and the merchant says they are not able to issue a refund due to the chargeback that their system doesn't allow to issuing refunds if that happens. So at the end of the day, I have to pay XXXX for nothing. The item was returned as the merchant requested, and they are stealing XXXX from me. The credit card company is now telling me they have gone past the time they can dispute this charge and there is nothing else they can do. I also have audio recording about the return and the merchant trying to say I have to get my refund through XXXX  which isnt true as they received the money.
